title: PSS Agent TOML Configuration
description: Schema for .agent.toml files generated by /pss-setup-agent. Defines the recommended skills, complementary agents, and metadata for a profiled agent. NOTE: A .agent.toml defines a reusable role-plugin profile, NOT a specific AI Maestro agent/persona. The same role-plugin can be assigned to many personas simultaneously. Do NOT add persona names, nicknames, or agent-specific identifiers to the profile.

name.description: Agent name (kebab-case). For Role-Plugins, this MUST match both the plugin name (from plugin.json) and the .agent.toml filename stem. This triple-match rule (plugin name == filename stem == [agent].name) is how AI Maestro identifies Role-Plugins.

source.description: How the agent was specified: 'path' for direct file path, 'plugin:<name>' or 'plugin:<owner>/<name>' for plugin notation

path.description: Absolute path to the <agent-name>.md definition file

effort.description: Model effort level for this agent (CC v2.1.78+). Controls thinking depth. `xhigh` is Opus 4.7's extension beyond `high` (CC v2.1.111+); non-Opus-4.7 models silently fall back to `high`.

maxTurns.description: Maximum number of turns the agent can take before stopping (CC v2.1.78+).

disallowedTools.description: Tools this agent is not allowed to use (CC v2.1.78+). Uses same syntax as settings disallowedTools.

description.description: Human-readable description of the agent role, used for plugin.json and README generation

text.description: Description of the role-plugin for README and plugin.json

requirements.description: Design requirements context used for profiling (empty if none provided)

files.description: Basenames of requirement files used for profiling

skills.description: Tiered skill recommendations, ordered by relevance within each tier

primary.description: Core skills the agent needs for daily work (min 1, max 7). Score >= 60% of max.

secondary.description: Useful skills for common tasks (max 12). Score 30-59% of max.

specialized.description: Niche skills for specific situations (max 8). Score 15-29% of max.

excluded.description: Skills considered but excluded. Keys are skill names, values are exclusion reasons. Stored as TOML comments in the file (not parsed, for human reference).

agents.description: Agents that complement this agent's workflow (spawned as helpers or used alongside)

recommended.description: Agent names that complement this agent

commands.description: Recommended slash commands for this agent

recommended.description: Command names that enhance this agent's workflow

rules.description: Rules that should be active when this agent runs

recommended.description: Rule names that enforce quality constraints for this agent

mcp.description: MCP servers that enhance this agent's capabilities

recommended.description: MCP server names that enhance this agent's capabilities

hooks.description: Hooks relevant to this agent's workflow

recommended.description: Hook configurations relevant to this agent's workflow

lsp.description: LSP servers relevant to this agent

recommended.description: LSP server names for languages this agent works with

output_styles.description: Output formatting styles for this agent

recommended.description: Output style names that customize this agent's output formatting

dependencies.description: External plugins, skills, or tools required for this agent to function. Used to validate that prerequisites are installed before creating the agent from the TOML.

plugins.description: Plugin names that must be installed (e.g., 'ai-maestro-integrator-agent', 'claude-ecosystem')

skills.description: Skill names from external plugins that must be available globally

mcp_servers.description: MCP server names that must be configured in settings

tools.description: CLI tools that must be on PATH (e.g., 'gh', 'docker', 'terraform')

scripts.description: CLI scripts that must be on PATH (e.g., 'amp-send.sh', 'aimaestro-agent.sh')

hooks.description: Hook configurations that must be installed globally (e.g., hook names from settings.json)

rules.description: Rule names that must be available

agents.description: Agent names that must be available

commands.description: Command names that must be available

lsp_servers.description: LSP server names that must be configured

output_styles.description: Output style names that must be available

metadata.description: Optional plugin provenance fields propagated into the generated plugin.json by /pss-make-plugin-from-profile. All fields are optional — emitted only when present.

userConfig.description: Optional userConfig section propagated verbatim into the generated plugin.json. Pass-through: PSS does NOT validate the nested structure. Consumers must follow the Claude Code plugins-reference.md userConfig schema (configurable settings exposed to end users when the plugin is installed). Example: { apiKey = { type = 'string', required = true } }

monitors.description: Optional monitors section propagated verbatim into the generated plugin.json (CC v2.1.105+). Background monitor plugin manifest key that auto-arms at session start or on skill invoke. Pass-through: PSS does NOT validate the nested structure. Consumers must follow the Claude Code plugins-reference.md monitors schema.

description.text: Security auditor agent that performs vulnerability scanning, code review, and compliance checks
Frontend developer agent specialized in React/Next.js web applications with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS

excluded.vue-frontend: Excluded: conflicts with React (requirements specify React/Next.js)
excluded.angular-dev: Excluded: conflicts with React (requirements specify React/Next.js)
